Impact Summary: Long downturn driven by feed volatility and drought; herd liquidation.
Military procurement altered domestic beef availability and pricing.
Energy spike raised fertilizer/feed costs; slowed herd expansion.
Consumer demand weakened; beef consumption declined relative to poultry.
Pasture losses forced herd liquidation; feedlot margins worsened.
High unemployment reduced beef demand; price volatility increased.
Import limits created price volatility in domestic markets.
Severe drought reduced forage; accelerated culling.
Corn/soymeal shortages raised feeding costs; reduced profitability.
Reduced export demand from key Asian markets.
Short-term restaurant demand decline; supply chain uncertainty.
Export bans from Japan/S. Korea; U.S. beef exports fell >80%.
Energy price volatility increased feed and transport costs.
Corn diverted to ethanol; feed prices spiked; herd contraction accelerated.
Consumer shift to cheaper proteins; restaurant beef demand fell.
Severe pasture loss; cattle inventory fell to multi-decade lows.
Tariffs disrupted beef export growth; price uncertainty increased.
Producers retained heifers; start of new expansion cycle.
Plant closures; slaughter backlog; retail prices up, rancher prices down.
Container shortages and port congestion slowed beef exports.
Forced liquidation of cows; U.S. herd reached 74-year low by 2025.
Record-high feed, fuel, fertilizer, labor costs; profitability pressure.
Energy and fertilizer price spikes increased production costs.
Four packers control ~80-85% of processing; amplifies shocks.
Repeated expansion/contraction cycles driven by climate and feed costs.
